articles.title: Heatwave likely to return to Rajasthan after short spell of relief - Awaz The Voice
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Jaipur
After a brief spell of rain brought temporary relief from soaring temperatures, large parts of Rajasthan are expected to experience intense heat once again from Saturday, with temperatures in some regions likely to climb up to 45 degrees Celsius in the coming days, according to the Jaipur Meteorological Centre.
Officials said western parts of the state are set to witness the return of heatwave conditions as dry weather and rising daytime temperatures begin to dominate once again.
In southeastern Rajasthan, maximum temperatures are expected to remain between 44 and 45 degrees Celsius over the next few days, increasing discomfort across several districts.
The weather department also forecast localized thunderstorm activity over the weekend.
Parts of the Bikaner division, the Shekhawati region, Jaipur, and northern areas of the Bharatpur division may experience thunderstorms on Saturday and Sunday, accompanied by gusty winds reaching speeds of 40 to 50 kilometres per hour.
Light rain or drizzle is also likely at isolated locations in these regions, officials said.
During the 24-hour period ending at 8:30 am on Saturday, weather conditions across most parts of the state remained largely dry, although heatwave conditions and unusually warm nights were reported from several areas.
Phalodi recorded the highest daytime temperature in the state at 44.8 degrees Celsius, according to the meteorological department.

articles.title: Heatwave conditions to return to Rajasthan after brief respite - newsdrum.in
articles.full_text:
Jaipur, May 16 (PTI) Intense heat is likely to grip most parts of Rajasthan again from Saturday, with maximum temperatures expected to touch 45 degrees Celsius in the coming days in some parts of the state, the Jaipur Meteorological Centre said.
Over the last few days, rain in Rajasthan had brought some respite from the heat.
Western Rajasthan is likely to witness renewed heatwave conditions from Saturday, it said.
In the southeastern parts of the state, maximum temperatures are expected to hover between 44 and 45 degrees Celsius over the next few days.
On Saturday and Sunday, parts of the Bikaner division, Shekhawati region, Jaipur and northern areas of the Bharatpur division may witness thunderstorms accompanied by winds with speeds reaching 40 to 50 kmph. Light rainfall or drizzle is also likely at isolated places, according to the weather office.
Weather conditions remained largely dry across Rajasthan over the last 24 hours, which ended at 8.30 am. Heatwave and warm night conditions were recorded at several locations during this period.
Phalodi recorded the highest maximum temperature in the state at 44.8 degrees Celsius, the meteorological centre said. PTI AG DIV DIV
